title: file limits not being honored?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Struggling to get a simple pod (rootless) and running into all kinds of problems that I am solving one by one. The latest one that has me stumped is I am running into a too many open files panic, where my hard and soft limits are `65535`.

The error is also a bit weird, but I understand why its saying that (note: the error output is not the real issue).

I have seen another bug with file permissions, but that was within the container, this seems to be from the host. If I run the same script from root, it works.

```
[me@localhost ~]$ ulimit -aH | grep files
open files                      (-n) 65535
[me@localhost ~]$ cat /etc/security/limits.d/20-nproc.conf
# Default limit for number of user's processes to prevent
# accidental fork bombs.
# See rhbz #432903 for reasoning.

*          soft    nproc     4096
*          soft    nofile    65535
*          hard    nofile    65535
root       soft    nproc     unlimited
```

**Describe the results you received:**


comments.body: Podman v1.6.4 is getting very long in the tooth - can you try with a more recent version and see if things are working there? We've done quite a bit with limits in the v2.0 releases.

How do I upgrade? I don't see anything when I check for upgrades on RHEL 7.9 (unfortunately can't upgrade to RHEL 8).

```
sudo yum upgrade -y
Loaded plugins: langpacks, product-id, search-disabled-repos, subscription-manager
No packages marked for update
```

You can install the latest versions for RHEL/Cent 7 from OBS here: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman

Note that these are not officially supported on the RHEL side. Cent/RHEL 7 official packages are not receiving further rebases of Podman and will only get security-related fixes.
Translation, time to upgrade to RHEL8/Centos 8 if you want to use newer stuff.
So I upgraded to RHEL 8, and the new AppStream version of container tools. I ran into another set of issues there, but I'll close this an investigate further.
